rulings.Case Title: BENBIHU KAHLID VS. BRISLEY BRITO et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, SEPTEMBER 12, 2007, LINE 9. DEFENDANT BRISLEY BRITO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM IS BARRED BY WORKERS COMPENSATION; WHICH IS TO BE LIBERALLY CONSTRUED IN FAVOR OF AWARDING COVERAGE FOR EMPLOYEES. ARRIAGA V COUNTY OF ALAMEDA (1995) 9 CAL. 4TH 1055, 1065. IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT PLAINTIFF REGULARLY SOUGHT EXTRA SHIFTS, THAT IS WHY HE WAS ON THE PREMISES AND SO THE INJURY OCCURRED IN THE COURSE AND SCOPE OF HIS WORK: GARDNER V INDUSTRIAL ACC. COMM. (1946) 73 CAL APP 2D, 361, 362 THROUGH 67, PRICE V WORKER'S COMPENSATION APPEALS BOARD (1984) 37 CAL. 3D 559. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN GOODMAN VS. THOMAS MCDONALD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Thomas Mcdonald'S Demurrer To First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, SEPTEMBER 12, 2007, LINE 11. DEFENDANT THOMAS MCDONALD'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT: HEARING REQUIRED. THE COURT ISSUES AN ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE ON OCTOBER 3, 2007 PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 128.7(2) TO PLAINTIFF AND HIS COUNSEL TO DEMONSTRATE WHY THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT DOES NOT VIOLATE C.C.P. SECTION 128.7(B) (1) (2) AND (3) GIVEN THE COURT'S EXPRESS RULING ON THE MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION THAT PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM IS BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. THE PARTIES ARE TO FILE SIMULTANEOUS BRIEFS ON OR BEFORE SEPTEMBER 26, 2007 TO ADDRESS (1) WHAT EVIDENCE FROM THE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION HEARING, IF ANY, MAY THE COURT CONSIDER IN RULING ON THE DEMURRER, AND, (2) WHETHER THE COURT'S FACTUAL RULINGS IN CONNECTION WITH PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR THE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION BAR PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM BASED ON THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. =(302/PJM/PM)
